description: A beginner-friendly course that builds a solid foundation in Kubernetes — from understanding why container orchestration exists, to deploying real applications using YAML manifests.
summary: Learn Kubernetes from the ground up: why it was created, how its architecture works, and how to deploy and expose applications using Pods, Deployments, Services, and YAML manifests.
target_audience: Absolute beginners to Kubernetes who have a basic familiarity with containers and Docker

lectures.title: What Is Kubernetes - Why Container Orchestration Matters
objectives: Understand the limitations of running services with containers alone (scaling, failure recovery, deployment)
Explain the problem Kubernetes solves and the core value it provides
Grasp the concept of orchestration intuitively through everyday analogies

lectures.title: Kubernetes Architecture - Control Plane and Worker Nodes
objectives: Distinguish the roles of the cluster, master (control plane), and worker nodes
Understand the function of key components: API Server, Scheduler, etcd, and kubelet
Trace the flow of a user command as it is processed through the cluster

lectures.title: Pods and Containers - The Smallest Deployable Unit in Kubernetes
objectives: Understand what a Pod is and how it differs from a container
Know the Pod lifecycle and single- vs. multi-container patterns
Create a Pod and inspect its status using kubectl

lectures.title: Deployments and Services - Deploying Apps and Exposing Them
objectives: Manage Pod replication, self-healing, and rolling updates with a Deployment
Understand how to access Pods using a Service (ClusterIP/NodePort)
Deploy a Deployment with kubectl and expose it via a Service

lectures.title: Deploying with YAML Manifests - Hands-On with Declarative Configuration
objectives: Understand the difference between imperative and declarative approaches
Write Deployment and Service YAML manifests from scratch
Apply manifests with kubectl apply and verify the results
